# QA Plan: Sprint 2 — M2 Content Engine (kickoff)
**Date**: 2026-06-10
**Generated by**: /qa-plan
**Scope**: 10 stories (level generator, scoring/stars, subtraction world, carryover QA)
**Engine**: Godot 4.6 (GDScript, gdUnit4)
**Sprint File**: `production/sprints/sprint-02.md`

> Note: stories are tracked in the sprint doc (no per-story `story-*.md` files), so
> Types are **inferred** from the sprint acceptance criteria. The generator's GDD
> (S2-001) and ADR (S2-002) are themselves sprint deliverables; until they land, the
> generator's testable requirements derive from **ADR-0003 (solvability invariant)**
> and `LevelData.is_solvable`. Re-derive precise cases from the GDD Formulas/Edge
> Cases sections once S2-001 is reviewed.

## Automated Tests Required

### S2-003a — Generator (by construction) — Logic  ⭐ keystone
**Test file**: `tests/test_level_generator.gd`
**What to test** (derive exact knobs from the S2-001 GDD once approved):
- **Solvability invariant (property test):** for **≥100 distinct seeds**, the generated
  `LevelConfig` satisfies `LevelData.is_solvable` — i.e. for every result,
  `card_count == 3 × occurrences in the target queue` (ADR-0003). This is the gate
  that proves the by-construction approach.
- The generator returns a fully-formed `LevelConfig` (card pool, layout, target queue)
  that `BoardModel.from_config` accepts without error.
- Node-free / deterministic: no `Node`, no I/O, no scene-tree access (ADR-0001).

**Edge cases**:
- Smallest viable level (minimum results / minimum cards).
- A result that appears multiple times in the queue (must still be exactly 3× per occurrence).
- Seed `0` and a large seed value.

**Estimated test count**: ~6–8 (incl. the seeded property loop)

---

### S2-003b — Generator difficulty knobs + determinism — Logic
**Test file**: `tests/test_level_generator.gd`
**What to test**:
- **Determinism:** same `(seed, knobs)` → byte-identical `LevelConfig` (assert on `to_dict`
  or field-by-field). Different seeds → different layouts (statistically).
- **Each difficulty knob changes output as specified** by the GDD (e.g. operand magnitude
  raises card values; # distinct results changes the result set size; queue length changes
  the queue). One focused test per knob.
- Solvability **still holds** across the knob ranges (fold into the property test with varied knobs).

**Edge cases**:
- Knob at minimum and maximum of its documented safe range.
- A knob combination the GDD flags as the hardest still produces a solvable level.

**Estimated test count**: ~1 per knob + 2 determinism = ~6

---

### S2-004 — Wire generator into level flow — Integration
**Test file**: `tests/test_level_generator_integration.gd`
**What to test**:
- `LevelData.get_level(n)` for `n` beyond the authored layouts returns a **generated**,
  solvable `LevelConfig` (seeded by level index → reproducible).
- A generated level is **playable end-to-end**: build the `BoardModel`, route the queue to a
  WIN with no illegal state (reuse the playthrough harness if practical).
- Authored levels 1–3 are unchanged (generator only kicks in past the authored range).

**Edge cases**:
- The boundary level (last authored → first generated) transitions cleanly.
- Level index determinism: `get_level(n)` twice returns the same level.

**Estimated test count**: ~4

---

### S2-011 — Efficiency score — Logic *(Nice-to-Have; lands deferred S1-021)*
**Test file**: `tests/test_scoring.gd`
**What to test** (per the S2-010 quick-design formula):
- Discards→stars thresholds at every boundary (e.g. 0 discards → 3★; threshold-1 / threshold
  values → the correct star count). Use the exact thresholds from the spec.
- Pure / deterministic; node-free.

**Edge cases**: 0 discards (max stars); a very high discard count (min stars, never < 1 on a win).

**Estimated test count**: ~5 (boundary-driven)

---

### S2-020 — Subtraction operation world — Integration *(Nice-to-Have)*
**Test file**: `tests/test_card_data.gd` (+ generator)
**What to test**: subtraction `CardData` produces correct results (incl. results that stay ≥ 0
per the design); the generator can emit solvable subtraction levels. Manual: themed skin renders.

---

## Manual QA Checklist

### S2-012 — Stars in ResultScreen — UI
**Verification**: manual step-through + screenshot · **Sign-off**: ui-programmer / designer
**Evidence**: WIN screenshot showing the star row populated.
- [ ] WIN shows the computed star count in the previously-reserved `StarRatingPlaceholder`.
- [ ] Star count matches the discard count per the S2-010 formula (cross-check with S2-011).
- [ ] No regression to the existing WIN layout (claim button, hero star, confetti).

### S2-021 — M1 carryover device sign-offs — Visual/Feel
**Verification**: on-device · **Sign-off**: qa-lead · **Evidence**: `production/qa/evidence/`
- [ ] JuiceService full-motion (clear burst / haptic / punch) reads correctly on a mid-range device.
- [ ] Sustained **60 FPS** during cascades on a mid-range device.
- [ ] AudioService SFX correct for each `GameEvent.Kind`; mute respected.
- [ ] Tutorial visual ACs: reduced-motion = no pulse/bob (AC15); colorblind ring/arrow distinct (AC16); banner no clip at base + max font scale (AC17).
- [ ] Settings/pause menu feel on device.

### S2-020 — Subtraction world skin — Visual
- [ ] Subtraction world skin renders distinctly from addition; numerals legible at small size.

---

## Smoke Test Scope
Before any QA hand-off this sprint (`/smoke-check sprint`):
1. Game launches to the board without crash.
2. A new game starts; level 1 plays.
3. **A generated level (index past the authored range) loads, is solvable, and plays to a WIN.**
4. Save/load round-trip intact (progression persists across restart).
5. Win/lose result screen appears and routes (advance / retry).
6. No new frame spikes during a generated-level cascade.

---

## Playtest Requirements
| Story | Goal | Min sessions | Player type |
|-------|------|--------------|-------------|
| S2-003a/b generator | Do generated levels feel solvable and fairly difficult (not degenerate / not trivial)? | 2–3 | mixed |

**Sign-off**: playtest notes → `production/session-logs/playtest-sprint-2-generator.md`, reviewed
by game-designer before the generator stories are marked Complete. *(Advisory — the automated
solvability property test proves correctness; playtest validates **feel/difficulty**, which it cannot.)*

---

## Definition of Done — Sprint 2
- [ ] All acceptance criteria verified — automated test OR documented manual evidence
- [ ] Test files exist for all Logic/Integration stories (generator, integration, scoring)
- [ ] **Solvability property test green (≥100 seeds)** + generator determinism test green
- [ ] Generator GDD (S2-001) **and** ADR (S2-002) exist and are reviewed
- [ ] Manual evidence for UI/Visual stories (stars screenshot; device sign-offs)
- [ ] `/smoke-check sprint` passes before QA hand-off
- [ ] No regressions; code reviewed (`/code-review`); stories → Complete (`/story-done`)
- [ ] `/team-qa sprint` sign-off: APPROVED or APPROVED WITH CONDITIONS
